food_name: Pempek (Palembang fish cake with cuko vinegar sauce)
food_subgroup: Fish and tapioca starch cake with dark sweet-sour dipping sauce

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Pempek. Closest parent is fish cake with starch binder and dipping sauce. No exact URI available; gap noted.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish and tapioca starch cake (Palembang pempek style) with palm sugar vinegar sauce
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0FRP is the closest FoodEx2 parent for fish-based processed cake products; no exact code exists for pempek with cuko sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for pempek or Palembang fish cake.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of pempek is approximately 200g, comprising 2–3 pieces of fish cake together with the cuko dipping sauce, providing around 336 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Pempek — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table; component-based estimation from Spanish mackerel fish cake and palm sugar vinegar sauce
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asia Regional Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single FAO/INFOODS SEA entry exists for pempek as a composite dish. Values are estimated from component-level data: Spanish mackerel (tenggiri) fish cake and cuko sauce (palm sugar, vinegar, garlic, chilli, tamarind). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-reference for mackerel and tapioca starch nutrient profiles. Values are consistent with published Indonesian food composition literature.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — analogous estimate: tapioca starch-based fish cake with palm sugar sauce; GI driven by tapioca starch binder (~GI 70) moderated by fish protein, and cuko sauce palm sugar (GI ~35–50)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pempek in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 60 (medium) is estimated from analogous foods: tapioca starch GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database), palm sugar GI ~35–50 (peer-reviewed literature), moderated by the high fish protein content which slows gastric emptying. Glycemic load per 200g serving = (60 × 19.5 × 2) / 100 = 23.4.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from salted fish paste and cuko sauce
hypertension.guidance: A 200g serving delivers approximately 870mg sodium (36% of the 2400mg daily limit), so individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and request reduced-salt cuko sauce where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 60) and significant glycemic load (GL 23.4 per 200g serving) driven by tapioca starch and palm sugar in cuko sauce
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit pempek to a single small serving (100–150g) and pair it with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response; the fish protein content provides some glycaemic moderation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.2g/100g) and beneficial omega-3 fatty acids from Spanish mackerel
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pempek is relatively low in saturated fat and provides omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids and vitamin D from Spanish mackerel, which are cardioprotective; however, the moderate sodium content warrants attention in patients with hypertensive cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 fatty acids and selenium from Spanish mackerel with anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The Spanish mackerel in pempek provides omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids and selenium (20mcg/100g), both of which have anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management; garlic in the cuko sauce also contributes allicin with anti-inflammatory activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pempek is the iconic street food of Palembang, South Sumatra, Indonesia — fish cakes made from finely minced Spanish mackerel (tenggiri) emulsified with tapioca starch, shaped into various forms, boiled, and then deep-fried to order. The most celebrated version is Pempek Kapal Selam ('submarine pempek') — a large oval cake stuffed with a whole raw egg before boiling, yielding a soft, chewy exterior with a rich egg centre. The defining feature of pempek is the cuko dipping sauce — a tangy-sweet-spicy dark syrup made from palm sugar, garlic, bird's eye chilli, vinegar, and tamarind, which transforms the mild fish cake into a complex flavour explosion of sweet, sour, savoury, and heat. Nutritionally, pempek provides 168 kcal per 100g with 11.5g protein from the mackerel, 19.5g carbohydrate primarily from tapioca starch, and only 1.2g saturated fat. The mackerel contributes omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin D (1.8mcg/100g), vitamin B12 (1.5mcg/100g), and selenium (20mcg/100g). The cuko sauce adds significant sugar (8.5g/100g) and sodium (435mg/100g). The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium), with a glycemic load of 23.4 per 200g serving. Pempek is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is now widely available across Indonesia and at Indonesian food stalls in Singapore, served as a snack or light meal at food courts and street food stalls.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for pempek as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA component-level data for Spanish mackerel fish cake and palm sugar vinegar sauce, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for mackerel and tapioca starch. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using tapioca starch and palm sugar GI values. Confidence is medium rather than high because no direct measurement of the composite dish exists in a primary approved source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for pempek as a composite dish (fish cake + cuko sauce) are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-13. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite dish including cuko sauce is not reported in available approved sources. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible for this preparation — no hydrogenated fats used). All core macros (protein_g 11.5, fat_g 5.5, carbohydrate_g 19.5) are non-zero and supported by component-level estimation from FAO/INFOODS SEA. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate based on tapioca starch GI ~70 and palm sugar GI ~35–50 from the University of Sydney GI Database, moderated by fish protein content. The input description field was truncated in the source record; the full description has been reconstructed from the llm_context field. Health suitability condition key updated from 'diabetes' to 'type2_diabetes' per Veritas v2 naming rules.
